response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad generalist background with strong web and distributed systems experience, plus tangential geospatial exposure via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role's core requirement is production-grade C++ or Rust for high-performance routing data pipelines, and his C++ experience is limited to a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js, and he has no experience with routing tiles, navigation guidance systems, or real-time traffic data integration — all central to this role.
strengths: Geospatial platform development experience through GISCollective and Green Map GIS application
Distributed systems experience at scale — handled millions of HTTP requests per second at Sociomantic with low-latency async I/O patterns
Linux familiarity and some historical C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in personal projects
concerns: C++ is not a primary skill — only 6 months of commercial C/C++ experience; no Rust experience listed anywhere
No routing, navigation tiling, or traffic signal integration experience — the GIS work is data collection for NGOs, not production routing pipelines
Career trajectory is firmly in TypeScript/Node.js web development, moving further from systems programming over time

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation domain experience
Adjacent geospatial and distributed systems background
